ps140: O most wise father George,* with honour you bowed down before the precious and all-pure icon of Christ,* strengthened by His power,* in no way fearing the vehemence of those who fought against God.* Entreat Him that He grant our souls* peace and great mercy.

O glorious father George,* you endured the anger of the impious* who were mindlessly enraged,* and whom you saw exalting themselves in impious arrogance,* and yet manifestly and utterly crushed by providence,* moved to defend the senseless.

O father George, as a fellow-labourer of God,* a priest and husbandman,* by your words you have now brought to the Master fruit a hundredfold:* the souls of the saved.* Pray now  that He grant to our souls* peace and great mercy.
theotokion: Rejoice, O bush unburned* Rejoice, you who alone gave birth to the Angel of great counsel!* Rejoice, will of the worshipful Trinity!* Rejoice, confirmation of mortals!* Rejoice, you who caused to spring forth the uncultivated Grain of paradise:* the King and Lord who causes* the offshoots of evil to wither.
stavrotheotokion: The unblemished ewe-lamb and Mistress,* seeing her Lamb upon the Cross,* bereft of form and beauty, said, lamenting:* Woe is me!* To where has Your comeliness departed, O You who are most sweet?* Where is Your goodness?* Where the shining grace of Your form,* O my Son most beloved?
